request.description: Login with the specified endpoint and storing the AccessToken, RefreshToken & UserId in our environment by automating our post-response script so that we can chain in our next response.

exec: console.log("The UserId generated from the response is : ", apiResponse.id)

request.description:
providing accessToken in bearer in Authorization from the AccessToken we got from our previous request.

The response will generate the the current user details which we logged in along with the id in json.

exec: console.log("The RefreshToken generated frommthe response is : ", apiResponse.refreshToken);

request.description: Extend the session and create a new access token without username and password. Tokens can also be passed using cookies.

item.description:
The **auth** endpoint provides details about the user authentication and authorization and refresh tokens.  
  
We can use any user's credentials from [dummyjson.com/users](https://dummyjson.com/users). Tokens are returned in the response and set as cookies.

item.name: Products

exec: console.log("The ProductId generaated from the response is: ", apiResponse.products[1].id);

request.description:
By default we will get 30 items.

I had automate my test script in post-response script we will extract product id and store it in environment so that I can use the variable value dynamically and can chain for my next responses.  
  
Also we need to pass the bearer token in authorization tab.

We will pass the productId captured with automated post response script which is saved in the environment.

We will get the info of the single product, here we will pass the ProductId dynamically in the api endpoint.  
  
Also we need to pass the bearer token in authorization tab.

We can search a product with this respective endpoint, and we can pass key as q and value (products ot title) as key-value pair as Query Params.

like {{BaseUrl}}/products/search -- is the endpoint  
q= lipstick ---- is the query params.

so full endpoint becomes -- {{BaseUrl}}/products/search?q=lipstick

query.description: select as query params with comma-separated values to select specific data

request.description:
You can pass limit and skip params to limit and skip the results for pagination, and use limit=0 to get all items.

You can pass select as query params with comma-separated values to select specific data

query.description: sort by is the key and the value could be field name
the value could be either asc or desc

request.description: You can pass sortBy and order params to sort the results, sortBy should be field name and order should be "asc" or "desc"
You can pass sortBy and order params to sort the results, sortBy should be field name and order should be "asc" or "desc"

body.raw:
{
	"title": "Nivia Storm Football",
	"description": "The Nivia Storm Football is a very authentic football it looks like volleyball, and when u will give a header it will feel like stone",
	"category": "football",
	"price": 15,
	"discountPercentage": 2.12,
	"rating": 4.64,
	"stock": 20,
	"tags": [
		"football",
		"stone"
	]
}

request.description:
Adding a new product will not add it into the server.  
It will simulate a POST request and will return the new created product with a new id.

exec: // As this are demo apis so when we add any product, the id generated is only for the api call, it doesn't really store the product in the database. So, when we try to update a product with a non-existing id, the api returns a 200 status code with a message "Product with id '195' not found"
// with existing id in the database of dummyJSON API
pm.test("Status code returned from the Api Request is 200", function () {
pm.test("The Response Status should be returned from the Api Request is OK", function () {

// pm.test("Status returned from the Api Request is Not Found", function () {
console.log("The ProductId generated from the response is successfully stored in the environment is : ", apiResponse.id);

request.description:
Deleting a product will not delete it into the server.  
It will simulate a DELETE request and will return deleted product with isDeleted & deletedOn keys.

item.description: The **products** endpoint provides a comprehensive dataset of sample product information, including details like names, prices, descriptions, images, and categories, ideal for testing and prototyping e-commerce applications.

item.name: Carts

request.description: getting carts by user id\[dynamic or static\]

request.description:
Adding a new cart will not add it into the server.  
It will simulate a POST request and will return the new created cart with a new id.

  
You can provide a userId and array of products as objects, containing productId & quantity.

exec: // As this are demo apis so when we add any product, the id generated is only for the api call, it doesn't really store the product in the database. So, when we try to update a product with a non-existing id, the api returns a 200 status code with a message "Product with id '195' not found"
// with existing id in the database of dummyJSON API
pm.test("Status code returned from the Api Request is 200", function () {
pm.test("The Response Status should be returned from the Api Request is OK", function () {
// If we pass the id generated in the previous add request we should get a 200 status code. If we pass a non-existing id we should get a 404 status code.
// pm.test("Status code returned from the Api Request is 404", function () {
// pm.test("Status returned from the Api Request is Not Found", function () {
console.log("The ProductId generated from the response is successfully stored in the environment is : ", apiResponse.id);

request.description:
Updating a cart will not update it into the server.  
It will simulate a PUT/PATCH request and will return updated cart with modified data.

Pass "merge: true" to include old products when updating.  

You can provide a userId and array of products as objects, containing productId & quantity.

// with existing id in the database of dummyJSON API

request.description:
Deleting a cart will not delete it into the server.  
It will simulate a DELETE request and will return deleted cart with isDeleted & deletedOn keys.

item.description: The **carts** endpoint offers a dataset of sample shopping cart data, including details like cart items, quantities, prices, and user IDs, useful for testing and prototyping e-commerce functionalities such as cart management and checkout processes.

item.description: The **users** endpoint provides a versatile dataset of sample user information and related data like carts, posts, and todos, making it ideal for testing and prototyping user management functionalities in web applications
